highlights.title: Choose how deeply the assistant responds
highlights.description: Use Fast response when you want a quick result, or switch to Deep verification when the assistant should verify with tools. One setting covers annotations, follow-ups, and co-reading tasks.

highlights.title: Organize collections from the keyboard
highlights.description: You can now drag items in the collection picker with the keyboard and hear announcements as their position changes. The existing button fallback remains available.

highlights.title: The Library fits its window better
highlights.description: From compact windows to wide screens, reading cards and loading skeletons now follow the space actually available, reducing crowding, empty gaps, and layout shifts.

highlights.title: The default theme feels more like paper
highlights.description: App surfaces, readers, annotations, distillations, discussions, and chat now share warm paper neutrals, warm ink, and a cinnabar accent for a more continuous reading experience.

highlights.title: Failed restores can return to the original data
highlights.description: A backup is now validated in a temporary copy before it replaces the current database. If switching fails, the app reopens the previous data from a rollback snapshot.

highlights.title: Safer imports, logs, and statistics
highlights.description: Web imports bind to validated network addresses, AI logs no longer include API key fragments, and thought and discussion totals stay accurate before annotation details load.
